title: On Captain Francis Grose.
content:
The devil got notice that Grose was a-dying,
        So whip! at the summons, old Satan came flying;
        But when he approach'd where poor Francis lay moaning,
        And saw each bed-post with its burden a-groaning,
        Astonish'd! confounded! cry'd Satan, "By ----,
        I'll want him, ere I take such a damnable load!"
meaning:
Robert Burns's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "On Captain Francis Grose."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
